title: Microstructural Modulation through Process Control Leading to Ultrastrong Ductile Fe–10Al–15Mn–0.8C–5Ni wt% Low-Density Steel
abstract_summary: This study investigates a low-density Fe–10Al–15Mn–0.8C–5Ni wt% austenitic steel processed via hot rolling, cold rolling, and annealing at 900 °C for 5–30 min. The key process control targets the refinement of δ-ferrite-derived B2 intermetallic stringer bands, which otherwise cause premature failure. Mechanical testing reveals a remarkable combination of ~1 GPa yield strength, ~28% total elongation, and a toughness of 1.72 kJ mol⁻¹, attributed to composite-like strain partitioning between the soft austenite matrix and hard B2 phase.
research_objective: To quantify the precipitation strengthening contribution of the B2 second phase, clarify the fracture behavior, and through systematic process parameter variation achieve delayed tensile failure to develop an ultrastrong ductile low-density steel.
main_process_variables: Hot rolling (1050 °C to 900 °C finish, 14 mm to 3 mm, 9 passes)
Cold rolling (room temperature, 3 mm to 2 mm)
Annealing at 900 °C for 5–30 min followed by water quenching
Tensile testing (ASTM E8, room temperature, gauge length 12.5 mm, crosshead speed 1 mm min⁻¹, rolling direction)
expected_information_axis: microstructure (phase fractions, B2 band morphology, recrystallization, precipitate size and distribution)
property (yield strength, ultimate tensile strength, elongation, toughness, strain hardening exponent)
mechanism (strain partitioning, local strain, GND density, crack nucleation)

key_sections.role: Background on low-density steel development, motivation for Fe–10Al–15Mn–0.8C–5Ni alloy and B2 strengthening, research objectives
Materials preparation (melting, homogenization, rolling, annealing), characterization techniques (OM, FESEM, TEM, EBSD), mechanical testing (tensile, interrupted tests) and computational methods

Microstructure evolution after rolling and annealing, precipitation of intra- and intergranular B2, tensile properties, fracture behavior, strain hardening analysis, strain localization and crack nucleation mechanisms
Summary of process-dependent microstructure–property correlations, key findings on B2 refinement, composite-like deformation, and toughness enhancement
